title: Telemedicine | Piel en Armonia
description: Remote dermatology consultation with clear triage, escalation, and continuity criteria in Quito.
heading: Dermatology telemedicine in Quito
lead: Telemedicine works best when it improves diagnosis clarity and helps you decide quickly, safely, and with confidence.

thesis.title: Online care when it truly adds value
thesis.body: We define what can be solved remotely, what must be monitored, and when in-clinic review is necessary.

statement.title: Useful telemedicine with clear medical limits

internalMessage.description: Context, risk, and progression are reviewed to decide whether remote continuity is enough or clinic evaluation is required.

blocks.title: How it works
items: You share relevant history and contextual clinical images.
We prioritize risk and estimate if remote resolution is appropriate.
We provide an initial plan, care guidance, and review timing when needed.
If warning signs appear, we escalate immediately to in-clinic consultation.

blocks.title: When it usually fits
items: Follow-up of active treatment plans with recent change
Initial guidance before traveling to clinic

blocks.title: When escalation is required
items: Rapidly changing, bleeding, or non-healing lesions
Insufficient response to the initial remote plan
Need for an in-clinic diagnostic procedure

initiatives.title: Visual triage
initiatives.copy: Preliminary reading through clinical images to prioritize risk and response timing.

initiatives.title: Flare control
initiatives.copy: Remote adjustments that reduce flare progression between reviews.

initiatives.title: In-clinic escalation
initiatives.copy: Referral to clinic when lesion behavior, pain, or progression needs direct examination.

initiatives.title: Quito continuity
initiatives.copy: Hybrid continuity for Quito patients with remote follow-up and timely in-clinic control.

bookingStatus.title: Online booking under maintenance
bookingStatus.description: The booking form returns soon. Until then, this route remains active for first clinical guidance.
